hero.name: Skovbye Sexology
hero.attribution: Signe Skovbye — clinical sexologist, intimacy coordinator, psychomotor therapist.

nameSection.roleLine: Clinical sexologist · intimacy coordinator · psychomotor therapist.
nameSection.roleSub: Trained psychomotor therapist (KP) and clinical sexologist (DACS). Works with solo, couples, poly, youth, elders — and with directors and performers handling intimacy on set.

chapter1.title: Therapy
chapter1.lede: For you, for you both. Solo, couples, poly. Embodied, conversational, shame-free. Your pace, your order, your body.

items.text: Boundaries are not a test. You don't have to pass anything here.
The body speaks first. I listen too — even where the words aren't.
Poly, queer, unsure, curious — there's room for every version of you.
Intimacy is not a performance. It's attention.
Your pace. Your order. Your body.

steps.title: Arriving
steps.body: We sit. You get to be in the room before you need to explain anything.

steps.title: Speaking aloud
steps.body: You share what's there. I ask questions but don't fill the space.

steps.title: Sensing
steps.body: Sometimes we stay with words. Other times we move — breath, body — always only what you say yes to.

steps.title: Moving on
steps.body: We close. You go home with a small shift — rarely finished, always underway.

forPersonal.label: Come by if you —
items: need to learn to say no before you can say yes
have come out as queer and want to explore what that means in your body
are in an open or polyamorous relationship and need a third voice
have had a boundary crossed — recently or long ago

chapter2.title: Consultancy services
chapter2.lede: For institutions, film productions, care services, and youth education. Teaching, intimacy coordination, counselling — always grounded in the same therapeutic foundation.

pillQuote: Clear lines about how we may and may not speak, touch, or act enable an authentic portrayal of intimate relationships on screen.

forWork.label: Write to me if you —
items: are a director or producer in need of an intimacy coordinator
work in care services and need to talk to residents about sexuality
are young and want education that's not only about biology

items.quote: I can recommend Signe Skovbye — she is deeply professional and a pleasure to work with.
[mock] Signe's presence on set made the difference between actors feeling safe and feeling asked to perform safety.
[mock] The care-staff workshop she ran changed how we talk to residents about intimacy. We'd been avoiding the topic for years.
[mock] As a couple we went in stuck. Signe didn't fix us — she taught us how to notice when we were about to get stuck again.
[mock] The youth workshop was the first time our students asked questions about desire and consent without flinching.
[mock] I came out as queer at 47 and had no idea what any of it meant in my body. Three sessions later I had a vocabulary I didn't know existed.
